title: Electrochemical screening of Lindane in natural origin, biological and industrial samples
author: Kirti Thakur
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Dr. H. S. Gour Vishwavidyalaya, Sagar-470 003, Madhya Pradesh, India 

  E-mail  : kspitre@rediffmail.com kirti_thakur9@yahoo.com 

  Manuscript received 29 August 2005, revised 13 March 2006, accepted 23 March 2006  

 Direct current polarographic method has been developed for the qualitative as well as quantitative analysis of Lindane (B.H.C.). Lindane produces a well-defined polarographic wave (DCP) in (0.1 M) tetramethyl ammonium bromide (TMAB), as supporting electrolyte (acetone used as solvent) at pH 10 ± 0.2 with E 1/2  = -0.995 V vs SCE. The wave height is found to be proportional to the concentration of Lindane in solution. The developed method has been standardised for the determination of B.H.C. in vegetables, soil, fruits, blood and soft drinks. The reliability of the observed data has been tested by subjecting it to statistical treatment.
